q: Ko Ko may have eaten the chocolate cake or else Htwe Htwe may have eaten it.
a: Either Ko Ko or Htwe Htwe may have eaten the chocolate cake.

q: He didn't say a word about it to his wife and he didn't say a word about it to his son.
a: He didn't say a word about it either to his wife or to his son.

q: Mya Mya may be with Papa in her study. She may be with May May in the kitchen.
a: Mya Mya may be either with Papa in her study or with May May in the kitchen.

q: Cho Cho sometimes comes here. Her sisters sometimes come here.
a: Either Cho Cho or her sisters sometimes come here.

q: Cho Cho may have come here or Moe Moe may have come here.
a: Either Cho Cho or Moe Moe may have come here.

q: Mi Mi is coming to the party. Her sisters are coming to the party.
a: Either Mi Mi or her sisters are coming to the party.

q: Mi Mi is coming to the party. Her sister is coming to the party.
a: Either Mi Mi or her sister is coming to the party.

q: You can have tea. You can have coffee.
a: You can have either tea or coffee.

q: The cat is under the table. The cat is behind the curtain.
a: The cat is either under the table or behind the curtain.

q: He may visit us either this weekend or next weekend.
a: He may visit us either this weekend or next weekend.

q: We can go to either the zoo or the museum.
a: We can go to either the zoo or the museum.

q: She left her wallet either at home or in the car.
a: She left her wallet either at home or in the car.

q: The keys are on the desk. The keys are in the drawer.
a: The keys are either on the desk or in the drawer.

q: My brother will drive us. My father will drive us.
a: Either my brother or my father will drive us.

q: He called me yesterday. He called me this morning.
a: He called me either yesterday or this morning.

q: They stayed at the guesthouse. They stayed at the hotel.
a: They stayed either at the guesthouse or at the hotel.

q: She can wear the red dress. She can wear the black dress.
a: She can wear either the red dress or the black dress.

q: I will email the report. I will hand it in personally.
a: I will either email the report or hand it in personally.

q: The manager will approve the proposal. The board of directors will make the final decision.
a: Either the manager or the board of directors will make the final decision on the proposal.

q: He should apologize to the teacher in person or send a formal email of apology.
a: He should either apologize to the teacher in person or send a formal email of apology.

q: We can reschedule the meeting for next Monday or conduct it through a Zoom call this Friday.
a: We can either reschedule the meeting for next Monday or conduct it through a Zoom call this Friday.
